docstring:
/**
 * @brief CRT shutdown helper that runs global destructors exactly once.
 *
 * Compiler-generated routine emitted into every ELF executable/shared
 * object by GCC. It is registered into the `.fini_array` section and
 * invoked by the dynamic loader/CRT during program (or DSO) teardown.
 *
 * Behavior:
 *  - Returns immediately if the static guard `completed.0` is already
 *    non-zero, ensuring destructors run at most once even if the
 *    finalization path is reached multiple times.
 *  - If `__cxa_finalize` was resolved (non-NULL), it is called with
 *    `__dso_handle` to run any C++ destructors and `atexit`/registered
 *    cleanup callbacks associated with this DSO.
 *  - Calls `deregister_tm_clones()` to undo the transactional-memory
 *    clone table registration performed at startup.
 *  - Sets `completed.0 = 1` so subsequent invocations are no-ops.
 *
 * @note Has no parameters and no return value; this is internal CRT
 *       glue and should never be called directly by user code.
 * @note The companion startup routine is `frame_dummy` /
 *       `register_tm_clones`; the matching guard variable
 *       `completed.0` and `__dso_handle` are also compiler-emitted.
 */
assumptions: This is the standard compiler-generated __do_global_dtors_aux; the guard flag at ctx+0x4150 is the well-known 'completed.0' byte used by GCC.
The check at ctx+0x3fd0 compared against a non-null value is the weak-symbol test for __cxa_finalize in the GOT; rewrote as `__cxa_finalize != NULL`.
The indirect call `0x11a0(*&[ctx+0x4008])` passes __dso_handle to __cxa_finalize — inferred from the canonical pattern emitted by GCC for this function.

Collapsed the two epilogue branches (L_1907 and fall-through) that both set completed.0 and call deregister_tm_clones() into a single code path; the only difference was whether __cxa_finalize was invoked, which becomes the if-guard.
Dropped the leading `nop` (endbr/alignment artefact) and the push/pop of rbp (frame setup artefact).
divergences: [low] other: Original compares *&[var0+0x3fd0] against the constant 0x3fd000000000 (a PIC-relative self-reference pattern produced by GCC for weak symbol tests). Rewriter modeled this as `__cxa_finalize != NULL`, which is the canonical idiom; semantically equivalent under the stated assumption.
[low] other: Dropped nop/endbr and rbp push/pop frame setup — these are non-observable artefacts.
[low] control_flow: Both branches in the original execute deregister_tm_clones() and set completed.0 = 1; merging them into a single tail is behavior-preserving.
